reasoning.typography: System sans (likely Inter) with green accent color on key phrases. Functional but lacks typographic personality — could be any dev tool. Scale relationships are competent but unremarkable.
reasoning.hierarchy: Hero headline does dominate with clear size contrast. Green 'Scale to millions' creates focal point. Feature cards have consistent hierarchy but nothing particularly bold or surprising.
reasoning.distinctiveness: Standard developer platform playbook: dark theme, logo parade, feature grid with icons. Swap 'Supabase' for 'Planetscale' or 'Railway' and structure holds perfectly. Green is the only distinguishing element.
reasoning.color: Restrained palette: dark background, white text, single green accent used sparingly (~3-5% surface area). Disciplined use of brand color without overdecorating. Grayscale dominates appropriately.
reasoning.density: Reasonable breathing room in hero, but feature cards feel template-padded with uniform spacing. Not particularly confident about density — playing it safe rather than making intentional spatial statements.
reasoning.voice: IA is pure SaaS stencil: headline promise → social proof logos → feature grid (Database/Auth/Functions/Storage/Realtime/Vector). No narrative arc, no Supabase-specific story structure. Generic 'what we offer' catalog.

winning_patterns: Green accent color used with restraint — dominates only where needed ('Scale to millions', CTAs)
Hero headline has genuine hierarchy — size differential creates clear entry point
Dark theme executed cleanly without falling into busy neon trap
Code/terminal aesthetic in feature cards feels authentic to developer audience
anti_patterns: Logo parade social proof is the most tired pattern in B2B SaaS (datashares/submagic/mozilla/github/1password)
Feature grid is templated uniformity — every card same size, same padding, same structure
IA follows paint-by-numbers SaaS structure with zero story or Supabase-specific POV
'Build in a weekend / Scale to millions' is aspirational copy that could apply to 50 platforms
Typography has no character — system sans doing system sans things
